Headline: Vanguard Slashes Fees on Dozens of Mutual Funds and ETFs
Summary: The company that pioneered low-cost investing is again reducing fees on its mutual funds and exchange-traded funds.  Vanguard said Monday that it has reduced expense ratios for 84 mutual fund and exchange-traded share classes across 53 funds.  “When investors keep more of what they earn, the benefits compound over the long term, helping our clients achieve their most important financial goals,” Salim Ramji, Vanguard’s chief cxecutive officer, said in a statement.

Headline: Equities Boom on Fed Interest Rate Cut
Summary: The resumption of Fed interest rate cuts on Wednesday, September 17, 2025, was met with strong investor enthusiasm. Of the $16.4 billion in net flows into ETFs on Wednesday, $14.63 billion flooded into equities globally. While domestic ETFs remained the primary focus, international exposures also benefited.

Headline: Investors Retreat From International, Turn to U.S. Equities Ahead of Rate Cut
Summary: The highly anticipated Federal Reserve interest rate meeting this week kept investors focused domestically Tuesday, September 16, 2025. A collective retreat from international equities turned instead to an influx into U.S. equities as an asset class. Strategies that benefited spanned the range of equity exposures, including growth, value, mid- and small-cap, as well as broad large-cap equity ETFs.
